```{r}
setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-AllenInstitute/Species/Evo-M1-Trait-Data/____Unpublished__ProjectKaskan")
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(purrr)
```
## Compare and combine data from Summer Alznati, Jack Gaston and Rachael Robinson's dissertations

# 1 Load csv final data used in dissertations
```{r}
# Read Jack's means
jack <- read_excel("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-AllenInstitute/Species/Evo-M1-Trait-Data/____Unpublished__ProjectKaskan/Jack_Gaston_dissertation_file_copies/Jack Gaston Raw Cortical Data.xlsx", sheet = "means")
jack <- as.data.frame(jack)

# Read Rachael's means
rachael <- read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-AllenInstitute/Species/Evo-M1-Trait-Data/____Unpublished__ProjectKaskan/Rachael_Robinson_dissertation_file_copies/Rachael_data.csv")
rachael <- as.data.frame(rachael)

# Read Summer's means
summer <- read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-AllenInstitute/Species/Evo-M1-Trait-Data/____Unpublished__ProjectKaskan/Summer_Alznati_dissertation_file_copies/data_analysis.csv")
summer <- as.data.frame(summer)
```

## 1.1 Compare Rachael and Summer which are very similar
```{r}
# 1.2  Remove the "Total_neocortex" column which is only in the "summer" dataset
summer_noneo <- select(summer, -Total_neocortex)

# 1.3 Comparison in different libraries 
library(compare)
compare(rachael, summer_noneo)

library(arsenal)
summary(comparedf(rachael, summer_noneo))

# 1.4 Write function to compare values between two data frames
compare_values <- function(df1, df2) {
  identical_values <- sapply(1:nrow(df1), function(i) {
    sapply(1:ncol(df1), function(j) {
      identical(df1[i, j], df2[i, j])
    })
  })
  return(identical_values)
}
comparison_values <- compare_values(rachael, summer_noneo)
comparison_df <- as.data.frame(comparison_values)
print(comparison_df)
```
## Conclusion: Rachael and Summer are the same although Summer also has Total neocortex added. Use Summer

## 2 Check Summer's work: check the difference between the sum of all areas and Total neocortex
# remove the last for rows from summer which are not need  "Visual_Acuity"        "Sound_localisation"   "Auditory_acuity_Low"  "Auditory_Acuity_High"
# check whether "MST...8" and "MST...28"  are the same structure.  
## check the images (TO DO LATER)
## check if there are any conflicts or if these columns can be merged?
```{r}
# which Species have non NA values for MST...8?
summer$Species[!is.na(summer$MST...8)]
summer$MST...8[summer$Species == "Galago_senegalensis"]
summer$MST...8[summer$Species == "Otolemur_garnettii"]
```

```{r}
# which Species have non NA values for MST...28?
summer$Species[!is.na(summer$MST...28)]
summer$MST...28[summer$Species == "Aotus_trivirgatus"]
summer$MST...28[summer$Species == "Macaca_mulatta"]
```
# Conclusion: there is an error in the data. Recompile to figure out if there are any others

# 3 Compare Summer to compiled Rachel data

# 3.1 To make comparisons edit summer_noneo by merging MST versions and removing behaviour variables
```{r}
# Merge columns "MST...28" and "MST...8"
summer_noneo$MST <- ifelse(!is.na(summer_noneo$MST...28), summer_noneo$MST...28, summer_noneo$MST...8)

#  Remove the columns "MST...28" and "MST...8" as well as "Visual_Acuity" "Sound_localisation" "Auditory_acuity_Low" "Auditory_Acuity_High"
summer_compare <- select(summer_noneo, -MST...28, -MST...8, -Visual_Acuity, -Sound_localisation, -Auditory_acuity_Low, -Auditory_Acuity_High)
```

# 3.2 Recompile from all Rachael's excel files, and compare values as a check
```{r}
# Read all the Sheets in the Excel files into a list of data frames
directory <- "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-AllenInstitute/Species/Evo-M1-Trait-Data/____Unpublished__ProjectKaskan/Rachael_Robinson_dissertation_file_copies/Brains data for animals"
excel_files <- list.files(directory, pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE)
# Function to import each sheet of an Excel file into a separate data frame
import_excel_sheets <- function(file) {
  # Get the names of all sheets in the Excel file
  sheet_names <- excel_sheets(file)
  # Read each sheet into a separate data frame
  sheet_dfs <- lapply(sheet_names, function(sheet) {
    read_excel(file, sheet = sheet)
  })
  # Remove the file extension ".xlsx" from the basename
  base_name <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file))
  # Combine the sheet names with the adjusted basename of the file
  df_names <- paste0(base_name, "__", sheet_names)
  # Name the list elements with combined names
  names(sheet_dfs) <- df_names
  return(sheet_dfs)
}
# Import all sheets of each Excel file into a list of data frames
df_list <- lapply(excel_files, import_excel_sheets)
# Flatten the list
df_list <- flatten(df_list)
# Rename dataframes in df_list to get rid of special characters
for (i in seq_along(df_list)) {
  new_name <- make.names(names(df_list)[i])
  if (new_name != names(df_list)[i]) {
    message(paste("Renaming dataframe", names(df_list)[i], "to", new_name))
    names(df_list)[i] <- new_name
  }
}
## Only include the Sheets where there is a column that refers to AREA CONVERTED To mm2 #include cm2because it actually looks like it is mm2
area_column_names <- c("AREA CONVERTED To mm2", "AREA CONVERTED TO (mm2)", "AREA CONVERTED To cm2")
has_area_column <- sapply(df_list, function(df) any(area_column_names %in% names(df)))
# Filter df_list to include only data frames with area columns
area_df_list <- df_list[has_area_column]
```
# For dataframes in area_df_list rename "AREA CONVERTED To mm2", "AREA CONVERTED TO (mm2)" or "AREA CONVERTED To cm2" to "Area.mm2" and "REGION (Kaskan 2005)" to "Region.Kaskan"
```{r}
# Define the new column names
new_area_column_name <- "Area.mm2"
new_region_column_name <- "Region.Kaskan"
# Function to rename columns
rename <- function(df) {
  # Rename AREA columns
  area_columns <- c("AREA CONVERTED To mm2", "AREA CONVERTED TO (mm2)", "AREA CONVERTED To cm2")
  names(df)[names(df) %in% area_columns] <- new_area_column_name
  # Rename REGION column
  names(df)[names(df) == "REGION (Kaskan 2005)"] <- new_region_column_name
  return(df)
}
# Apply the function to each data frame in area_df_list
updated_df_list <- lapply(area_df_list, rename)
```

# Extract the column 'Information about brain' along with the following column from dataframes in updated_df_list. Store these dataframes in a new list called updated_df_list_information, while retaining the same dataframe names.
```{r}
# Create an empty list to store dataframes
updated_df_list_information <- vector("list", length(updated_df_list))
# Assign the names of the dataframes to the list
names(updated_df_list_information) <- names(updated_df_list)
# Iterate through each dataframe in updated_df_list
for (df_name in names(updated_df_list)) {
  # Get the dataframe from updated_df_list
  df <- updated_df_list[[df_name]]
  # Copy "Information about brain" column and the column after it
  if ("Information about brain" %in% names(df)) {
    info_index <- which(names(df) == "Information about brain")
    next_column_index <- info_index + 1
    # Check if the next column exists
    if (next_column_index <= ncol(df)) {
      # Create a new dataframe with the two columns
      updated_df_list_information[[df_name]] <- df[, c(info_index, next_column_index), drop = FALSE]
    }
  }
}
```
# Transpose all the data frames in the list updated_df_list_information, # Make dataframe
```{r}
# Transpose all data frames in updated_df_list_information and remove row names
transposed_df_list <- lapply(updated_df_list_information, function(df) {
  transposed_df <- as.data.frame(t(df))
  rownames(transposed_df) <- NULL
  return(transposed_df)
})
```
# Rename columns, delete NA columns, delete Row 1
```{r}
# Initialize an empty list to store modified data frames
info_df_list <- list()
# Loop through each data frame
for (df_name in names(transposed_df_list)) {
  # Get the current data frame
  df <- transposed_df_list[[df_name]]
  # Set the first row to be column names
  colnames(df) <- df[1, ]
  # Identify columns with non-NA values
  non_na_cols <- apply(df, 2, function(x) any(!is.na(x)))
  # Subset the data frame to include only non-NA columns
  df <- df[, non_na_cols, drop = FALSE]
  # Delete Row 1
  df <- df[-1, ]
  # Reset Row numbers
  rownames(df) <- NULL
  # Store the modified data frame in the new list
  info_df_list[[df_name]] <- df
}
```
# Rename updated_df_list dataframes after these data: 
```{r}
# Rename dataframes in updated_df_list based on values in info_df_list
for (i in seq_along(updated_df_list)) {
  # Get the corresponding dataframe from updated_df_list
  df <- updated_df_list[[i]]
  
  # Get the corresponding values from info_df_list
  col1_value <- info_df_list[[i]][1]
  col2_value <- info_df_list[[i]][2]
  col3_value <- info_df_list[[i]][3]
  
  # Construct the new dataframe name
  new_df_name <- paste0(col1_value, "__", col2_value, "__", col3_value)
  
  # Rename the dataframe
  names(updated_df_list)[i] <- new_df_name
}
```

# Trim the dataset
```{r}
# Create a new list to store trimmed data frames
trimmed_df_list <- lapply(updated_df_list, function(df) {
  # Keep only the columns "REGION MEASURED", "Area.mm2" and "Region.Kaskan"
  df <- df[, c("REGION MEASURED", "Region.Kaskan", "Area.mm2"), drop = FALSE]
  return(df)
})
```
# Convert the list of data frames into a single data frame
```{r}
combined_df <- do.call(rbind, Map(cbind, Name__Source__Page = names(trimmed_df_list), trimmed_df_list))

# Reset row names
rownames(combined_df) <- NULL

# Split the column "Name__Source__Page" into two columns at the first occurrence of "__"
combined_df <- separate(combined_df, Name__Source__Page, into = c("Name", "Source__Page"), sep = "__", extra = "merge")
combined_df
```
# Rename to match species in final dataset
```{r}
# Create a lookup table for matching species names
species_mapping <- c(
  "Callithrix jacchus common marmoset" = "Callithrix_jacchus",
  "Cheirogaleus medius" = "Cheirogaleus_medius",
  "Tenrec" = "Echinops_telfairi",
  "Erinaceus europaeus" = "Erinaceus_europaeus",
  "Quoll" = "Dasyurus_hallucatus", ##check this
  "North American opossum" = "Didelphis_marsupialis",
  "Galago senegalensis" = "Galago_senegalensis",
  "Homo sapiens" = "Homo_sapiens",
  "Macaca sp. macaque" = "Macaca_mulatta", ##check this
  "Short tailed opossum" = "Monodelphis_domestica",
  "Mus musculus" = "Mus_musculus",
  "Mustela putorius" = "Mustela_putorius",
  "Nannospalax galili" = "Nannospalax_galili",
  "Slow loris" = "Nycticebus_coucang" ,
  "Platypus" = "Ornithorhynchus_anatinus",
  "Otolemur garnetti" = "Otolemur_garnettii",
  "Clifornia ground sqirrel" = "Otospermophilus_beecheyi",
  "Pteropus poliocephalus" = "Pteropus_niger", ##check this
  "Rattus sp." = "Rattus_norvegicus", ##check this
  "Tamarin" = "Saguinus_fuscicollis",
  "Squirrel Monkey" = "Saimiri_sciureus",
  "Sciurus carolinensis" = "Sciurus_carolinensis",
  "Fat tailed Dunnart" = "Sminthopsis_crassicaudata",
  "Echidna" = "Tachyglossus_aculeatus",
  "Tree Shrew" = "Tupaia_belangeri"
)

# Create a new column with the updated species names
combined_df$Species <- species_mapping[combined_df$Name]
```

# Rename to match areas in final dataset ## This is IN PROGRESS
```{r}
# Create a lookup table for matching area names -- needs to be implemented before averaging
area_mapping <- c(
  "1" = "1",
  "Aud cortex --> same features as 1 in other species" = "1",
  "2" = "2",
  "3a" = "3a",
  "A1" = "A1",
  "AI" = "A1",
  "A1+R" = "A1+R",
  "AI+R" = "A1+R",
  "CT" = "CT",
  "DL" = "DL",
  "DM" = "DM",
  "FEF" = "FEF",
  "FR" = "FR",
  "FST" = "FST",
  "FV" = "FV",
  "M1" = "M1",
  "MST" = "MST",
  "mst" = "MST",
  "MT" = "MT",
  "PV" = "PV",
  "R" = "R",
  "S1/3b" = "S1/3b",
  "3b" = "S1/3b",
  "3b =S1" = "S1/3b",
  "3b/S1" = "S1/3b",
  "SII" = "SII",
  "SII+PV" = "SII+PV",
  "PV+SII" = "SII+PV",
  "SII/Pv" = "SII+PV",
  "SMA" = "SMA",
  "Species" = "Species",
  "V1" = "V1",
  "VI" = "V1",
  "V2" = "V2",
  "VII" = "V2",
  "VP" = "VP",
  "VPP" = "VPP",
  "VPp" = "VPP",
  "Vr" = "Vr"
)

# Create a new column with the updated area names
combined_df$Region_Kaskan <- area_mapping[combined_df$Region.Kaskan]
```

# Filter out NA and zero value rows
```{r}
# Remove rows where "Area.mm2" is NA or zero
filtered_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Area.mm2) & Area.mm2 != 0)
```

# Calculate averages and create the new long dataframe
```{r}
# Group by "Species", "Region_Kaskan", calculate mean of "Area.mm2", and concatenate unique values of "Source__Page"
surfaceareas_long <- filtered_df %>%
  group_by(Species, Region_Kaskan) %>%
  summarize(Area.mm2 = mean(Area.mm2),
            Source__Page = paste0(unique(Source__Page), collapse = "___"))
surfaceareas_long
write_csv(surfaceareas_long, "surfaceareas_long.csv")
```
# Convert to wide dataframe
```{r}
surfaceareas_wide <- arrange(pivot_wider(surfaceareas_long, id_cols = Species, names_from = Region_Kaskan, values_from = Area.mm2), Species)
write_csv(surfaceareas_wide, "surfaceareas_wide.csv")
```

# Find columns in surfaceareas_wide but not in summer_compare
```{r}
surfaceareas_unique_cols <- setdiff(colnames(surfaceareas_wide), colnames(summer_compare))
sort(surfaceareas_unique_cols)
```

# include only the columns common to surfaceareas_wide and summer_compare
```{r}
common_cols <- intersect(colnames(surfaceareas_wide), colnames(summer_compare))
surfaceareas_compare <- surfaceareas_wide[, common_cols]
```

```{r}
sort(colnames(surfaceareas_compare))
```

```{r}
sort(colnames(summer_compare))
```

```{r}
# # 1.3 Comparison in different libraries 
# library(compare)
# compare(surfaceareas_compare, summer_compare)
# 
# library(arsenal)
# summary(comparedf(surfaceareas_compare, summer_compare))
# 
# # 1.4 Write function to compare values between two data frames
# compare_values <- function(df1, df2) {
#   identical_values <- sapply(1:nrow(df1), function(i) {
#     sapply(1:ncol(df1), function(j) {
#       identical(df1[i, j], df2[i, j])
#     })
#   })
#   return(identical_values)
# }
# comparison_values <- compare_values(surfaceareas_compare, summer_compare)
# comparison_df <- as.data.frame(comparison_values)
# print(comparison_df)
```

# get the sum of the columns from summer: "V1", "V2", "MT", "DM", "DL", "FST", "MST...8", "VPP", "VP", "CT", "Vr", "A1", "R", "A1+R", "S1/3b", "SII", "3a", "PV", "1",  "2", "SII+PV", "M1", "FEF", "FV", "SMA", "FR", "MST...28"  
## 3 Calculate as percentages and compare to Krubitzer percent neocortex tables 
## 4 Compare Summer to Jack
